channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 3 convicted for illegal BASE jumping in Yosemite
item.description: Three people have been convicted of illegal BASE jumping in Yosemite National Park in recent weeks, the park announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: John Houghton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: In emotional speech, Zohran Mamdani defends Muslim identity against 'racist and baseless' attacks
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Zohran Mamdani, the Democratic nominee for New York City mayor, pledged Friday to further embrace his Muslim identity in response to growing attacks by former Gov. Andrew Cuomo and his surrogates that he characterized as “racist and baseless.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Reagan ad that infuriated Trump set to run during World Series
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford said on Friday that the anti-tariffs advertisement featuring former President Reagan, which angered President Trump, will air during the World Series before being paused on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, WGN 9

item.title: New York adds $11M to hunger programs amid SNAP cuts, government shutdown
item.description: Governor Kathy Hochul announced $11 million in state funding for emergency food relief programs and urged the USDA to release $700 million in SNAP benefits to prevent a public health crisis, while the Fiscal Policy Institute suggested the state use its budget surplus to backfill the lost benefits.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pentagon sends largest aircraft carrier to Caribbean amid boat strikes
item.description: Hegseth’s order comes as the Trump administration has been striking alleged drug-smuggling boats in the Caribbean Sea.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Cholesterol medication recalled over 'failed dissolution specifications': What to know
item.description: “In some instances, stopping your medicine may be more harmful to your health than continuing to take the recalled medicine,” the FDA says.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Judge halts death penalty case in New Mexico due to federal shutdown
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — A judge in New Mexico has halted a federal death penalty case, citing the pinch of the U.S. government shutdown and a funding shortfall for specialized public defenders that stretches back to the summer.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Iron Maiden returns to Mexico City as part of 2026 'Run for Your Lives' tour
item.description: The iconic Iron Maiden rock band is bringing its "Run For Your Lives" tour to Mexico City next year, the group first visited Mexico 34 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: Body found during search for missing Vermont college student Lia Smith near Middlebury campus
item.description: Body discovered during search for missing Vermont college student Lia Smith, 21, who vanished from Middlebury College campus. Autopsy scheduled to determine identity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Inflation hit 3 percent in September as energy prices squeezed households
item.description: Annual inflation rose to 3% in September as a jump in gasoline and energy prices pushed price growth to its highest level since January.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, WFLA, NBC

item.title: SNAP benefits may run out Nov. 1. Here's how you could still get food assistance
item.description: A disruption or delay could impact 42 million people, or 1 in 8 Americans, who rely on SNAP to buy food.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: The Latest: US strikes 2 more alleged drug-carrying boats, this time in the Pacific Ocean
item.description: The U.S. military on Wednesday launched its ninth strike against an alleged drug-carrying vessel, killing three people in the eastern Pacific Ocean, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said, expanding the Trump administration’s campaign against drug trafficking in South America.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trail Blazers’ Billups, Heat’s Rozier among dozens charged in Mafia-linked gambling probe
item.description: Portland Trail Blazers head coach Chauncey Billups and Miami Heat guard Terry Rozier are among more than 30 people who have been charged in connection with schemes involving illegal sports betting and rigged poker games backed by Mafia, authorities said on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What is 'ghost tapping?': New scheme targeting tap-to-pay users
item.description: The Better Business Bureau is warning tap-to-pay users of a new scheme that could make simple transactions costly.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New US citizenship test proving difficult for immigrants
item.description: A new citizenship test that was rolled out this week by the Trump administration is being criticized as too difficult and could deter people from becoming U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: COVID-19 vaccines may help some cancer patients fight tumors
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The most widely used COVID-19 vaccines may offer a surprise benefit for some cancer patients – revving up their immune systems to help fight tumors.
item.dc:creator: LAURAN NEERGAARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sheriff's deputy got mad and fatally shot Sonya Massey without justification, prosecutor says
item.description: PEORIA, Ill. (AP) — The Illinois sheriff's deputy who killed Sonya Massey in her Springfield home last year eschewed his training and the principles of policing when he shot the Black woman who had called 911 for help, a prosecutor said Wednesday in Sean Grayson's murder trial.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Latest: North Carolina lawmakers approve map adding a GOP House seat
item.description: North Carolina lawmakers gave final approval Wednesday to a revised U.S. House map backed by President Donald Trump that is intended to help Republicans win an additional seat in next year’s elections. The new congressional map reshapes the state’s only current swing district, held by Democratic U.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Vendors on NYC's Canal Street say they were harassed and asked to show papers in immigration sweep
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A day after a mass of federal agents questioned street vendors and sparked protests on Manhattan's Canal Street, sellers were scarce on the busy strip. Some who did venture out Wednesday, though, were disheartened or riled up by a sweep in which they said people, including U.S.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Korea has stolen billions in cryptocurrency and tech firm salaries, report says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — North Korean hackers have pilfered billions of dollars by breaking into cryptocurrency exchanges and creating fake identities to get remote tech jobs at foreign companies, according to an international report on North Korea's cyber capabilities.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: USDA is reopening some 2,100 offices to help farmers access $3B in aid despite the ongoing shutdown
item.description: The Agriculture Department will reopen about 2,100 county offices all across the country Thursday despite the ongoing government shutdown to help farmers and ranchers get access to $3 billion of aid from existing programs.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 egg brands recalled for possible salmonella contamination
item.description: A recall issued last week has now expanded to include two brands of eggs that may be contaminated with salmonella.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WGN 9

item.title: Man taken into custody after driving car into White House gate: Secret Service
item.description: A man was taken into custody late Tuesday after driving his car into a portion of the White House security gate, a Secret Service official said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Task force aims to move those displaced by ex-typhoon in Alaska from shelters to hotels, rentals
item.description: Officials have identified more than 1,000 available hotel rooms plus additional rental options in Alaska's largest city for the hundreds of people displaced to Anchorage after the remnants of a typhoon ravaged their villages earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: During cold and flu season, the youngest kids really are the germiest
item.description: Forget colorful leaves. Any caregiver knows that the real signs of fall are kids with coughs, sneezes and sniffles. Autumn marks the start of respiratory virus season, when colds, flu and other bugs start circulating — especially among the very young.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Parties agree to extension banning deployment of National Guard troops to Illinois, but Supreme Court decision looms
item.description: CHICAGO (AP/WGN) — A federal judge in Chicago has agreed to extend a temporary restraining order banning the deployment of National Guard troops to Illinois by 30 days, hours before a temporary restraining order blocking the deployment was set to expire. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli settlers beat Palestinian farmers on video as attacks mount during West Bank olive harvest
item.description: RAMALLAH, West Bank (AP) — Israeli settlers descended on Palestinian olive harvesters and activists this week in the Israeli-occupied West Bank, beating them with clubs in an attack Palestinian health officials said sent at least one woman to the hospital with serious injuries.
item.dc:creator: JALAL BWAITEL and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Immigration agents conducting sweep on NYC's famed Canal Street confronted by protesters
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — An immigration enforcement sweep targeting vendors on Manhattan’s famed Canal Street turned chaotic on Tuesday after droves of angry New Yorkers surrounded federal agents and attempted to block them from driving off, prompting arrests and fierce standoffs along a bustling downtown co
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Democrats look to long term as North Carolina GOP redistricting plan seeks another seat for Trump
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Democrats rallied Tuesday against a new U.S. House map proposed by North Carolina Republicans that would likely reap another GOP seat at President Donald Trump's behest, but the critics acknowledged they'll likely be unable to halt the redraw for now.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA and GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal prosecutors charge man accused of threatening to shoot up Atlanta airport
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Federal prosecutors on Tuesday brought charges against a Georgia man who was arrested a day earlier by police after his family contacted authorities saying he planned a shooting at Atlanta's international airport.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: These are America’s safest – and most dangerous – cities, according to WalletHub
item.description: Threats ranging from public health and natural disasters to financial harm are among the many factors Americans face when they consider moving to a different city.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Texas couple accused of moving, leaving child behind
item.description: A 12-year-old Texas boy came home from school to find it empty and his family gone, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Roland Richter, Fox 5

item.title: A United Airlines emergency landing likely caused by collision with a weather balloon
item.description: A United Airlines plane appears to have collided with a weather balloon while cruising over Utah at 36,000 feet last week, fracturing layers of its windshield and forcing an emergency landing.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: San Francisco leaders push back against Trump's National Guard threat
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — President Donald Trump keeps threatening to send National Guard troops to San Francisco next, blasting the California city as crime-ridden and saying its residents are clamoring for federal help.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man who sent 'So I raped you' message sentenced for 2013 campus assault
item.description: The sentence came more than a year after Ian Cleary was extradited back to Pennsylvania from France over the assault at Gettysburg College, and nearly 12 years after the victim first went to police.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, Fox 5

item.title: NY police seek woman for questioning after baby is abandoned at Penn Station subway stop
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City police on Tuesday released images of a woman wanted for questioning after an infant girl with her umbilical cord still attached was abandoned at a busy midtown Manhattan subway station.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Authorities say remains found in Philadelphia are of missing woman Kada Scott
item.description: Remains found buried in a wooded area behind an abandoned school are those of Kada Scott, a 23-year-old woman who had been missing for about two weeks, prosecutors in Philadelphia said Monday.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4
